title: ferray-core: no einsum in the workspace
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's np.einsum is one of its most powerful functions — it handles arbitrary tensor contractions, traces, outer products, batch matrix multiplication, and reductions via Einstein summation notation. It lives in numpy._core (einsumfunc.py / c_einsum in C). ferray has no einsum anywhere in the workspace. This is a significant gap for users coming from NumPy, especially in scientific computing and ML preprocessing. Implementation options: (1) parse einsum notation at compile time via proc macro, (2) runtime string parsing like NumPy, (3) both. The core contraction loop should use BLAS gemm where possible.
